subject: Chemistry
title: Moseley Fixes the Table
description: A quick, practical guide to Moseley Fixes the Table.

hook.text: In 1913, Henry Moseley discovered that the true order of elements is atomic number, not atomic weight.

buildup.text: Mendeleev had ordered elements by weight, but some pairs seemed out of place. Tellurium and iodine didn't fit.

discovery.text: Moseley shot X-rays at elements and found each had a unique frequency tied to its number of protons.

twist.text: Moseley was just 25. A year later, he died at Gallipoli in World War I—a devastating loss for science.

climax.text: His work fixed every misplacement in the table and told chemists exactly how many elements remained to find.

punchline.text: Atomic number, not weight. Moseley perfected the table at 25.

quiz.question: What did Moseley discover about the periodic table?
options: Elements should be ordered by atomic number, not weight
That all elements emit the same X-rays
